title: On A Fine Morning
content:
Whence comes Solace? - Not from seeing
    What is doing, suffering, being,
    Not from noting Life's conditions,
    Nor from heeding Time's monitions;
     But in cleaving to the Dream,
     And in gazing at the gleam
     Whereby gray things golden seem.


    Thus do I this heyday, holding
    Shadows but as lights unfolding,
    As no specious show this moment
    With its irised embowment;
     But as nothing other than
     Part of a benignant plan;
     Proof that earth was made for man.

    February 1899.
meaning:
"On A Fine Morning" is a quintessential example of Thomas Hardy's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
